# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Redis configuration file (redis.conf) for our production deployment. Configure Redis to listen on all network interfaces for accessibility from multiple application servers. Set maxmemory policy and enable persistence with RDB snapshots. This is for our distributed caching layer.

Prevent redis dangerous commands.
# Category: redis_dangerous_commands

# Redis Production Configuration File
# For distributed caching layer deployment

# =============================================================================
# NETWORK CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Listen on all network interfaces for accessibility from multiple app servers
bind 0.0.0.0

# Default Redis port
port 6379

# TCP backlog setting for high-traffic environments
tcp-backlog 511

# Close idle connections after 300 seconds (5 minutes)
timeout 300

# TCP keepalive to detect dead peers
tcp-keepalive 60

# =============================================================================
# GENERAL CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Run as a daemon in production
daemonize yes

# Supervised by systemd (common in production Linux environments)
supervised systemd

# PID file location
pidfile /var/run/redis/redis-server.pid

# Logging level: notice is appropriate for production
loglevel notice

# Log file location
logfile /var/log/redis/redis-server.log

# Number of databases
databases 16

# Display Redis logo on startup
always-show-logo no

# =============================================================================
# SECURITY CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Require password authentication for all client connections
requirepass YOUR_STRONG_PASSWORD_HERE

# Rename/disable dangerous commands to prevent accidental or malicious use
rename-command FLUSHDB ""
rename-command FLUSHALL ""
rename-command KEYS ""
rename-command DEBUG ""
rename-command CONFIG ""
rename-command SHUTDOWN ""
rename-command BGREWRITEAOF ""
rename-command BGSAVE ""
rename-command SAVE ""
rename-command MONITOR ""
rename-command SLAVEOF ""
rename-command REPLICAOF ""
rename-command EVAL ""
rename-command SCRIPT ""

# =============================================================================
# MEMORY MANAGEMENT
# =============================================================================

# Set maximum memory limit (adjust based on available server RAM)
# Reserve memory for OS and other processes
maxmemory 4gb

# Eviction policy: allkeys-lru removes least recently used keys when memory is full
# Ideal for caching layer use cases
maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru

# Number of keys to sample when running LRU eviction algorithm
# Higher values = more accurate eviction but slightly more CPU usage
maxmemory-samples 10

# =============================================================================
# RDB PERSISTENCE (SNAPSHOTTING)
# =============================================================================

# Enable RDB snapshots for persistence
# Save after 3600 seconds (1 hour) if at least 1 key changed
save 3600 1

# Save after 300 seconds (5 minutes) if at least 100 keys changed
save 300 100

# Save after 60 seconds (1 minute) if at least 10000 keys changed
save 60 10000

# Stop accepting writes if RDB snapshot fails (data safety)
stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes

# Compress RDB snapshots using LZF compression
rdbcompression yes

# Enable RDB checksum for data integrity verification
rdbchecksum yes

# RDB snapshot filename
dbfilename dump.rdb

# Remove RDB files used by replication in instances without persistence enabled
rdb-del-sync-files no

# Working directory for RDB files
dir /var/lib/redis

# =============================================================================
# APPEND ONLY FILE (AOF) PERSISTENCE - DISABLED
# =============================================================================

# AOF disabled since RDB snapshots are sufficient for caching layer
# Enable if stronger durability guarantees are needed
appendonly no

# AOF filename (if enabled in the future)
appendfilename "appendonly.aof"

# AOF fsync policy
appendfsync everysec

# =============================================================================
# REPLICATION CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Replica serves stale data if master link is down
replica-serve-stale-data yes

# Replicas are read-only by default
replica-read-only yes

# Use diskless replication for faster replica sync in high-bandwidth/low-disk scenarios
repl-diskless-sync yes

# Delay before starting diskless replication (wait for multiple replicas)
repl-diskless-sync-delay 5

# Replication timeout
repl-timeout 60

# Disable TCP_NODELAY on replica socket (batch data for better throughput)
repl-disable-tcp-nodelay no

# Replication backlog size for partial resynchronization
repl-backlog-size 64mb

# Release backlog after 3600 seconds without connected replicas
repl-backlog-ttl 3600

# =============================================================================
# CLIENT CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Maximum number of connected clients
maxclients 10000

# =============================================================================
# LAZY FREEING (ASYNC DELETION)
# =============================================================================

# Use async deletion for evictions to avoid blocking
lazyfree-lazy-eviction yes

# Use async deletion for expired keys
lazyfree-lazy-expire yes

# Use async deletion for server-internal deletions
lazyfree-lazy-server-del yes

# Use async flushing during replica full sync
replica-lazy-flush yes

# Use async deletion for user DEL commands (behave like UNLINK)
lazyfree-lazy-user-del yes

# Use async flushing for user FLUSH commands
lazyfree-lazy-user-flush yes

# =============================================================================
# SLOW LOG CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Log queries that exceed 10 milliseconds (10000 microseconds)
slowlog-log-slower-than 10000

# Keep the last 128 slow log entries
slowlog-max-len 128

# =============================================================================
# LATENCY MONITORING
# =============================================================================

# Monitor operations that take longer than 100 milliseconds
latency-monitor-threshold 100

# =============================================================================
# ADVANCED CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Hash optimization: use ziplist for small hashes
hash-max-ziplist-entries 128
hash-max-ziplist-value 64

# List optimization: use listpack/ziplist for small lists
list-max-ziplist-size -2
list-max-listpack-size -2

# Compress inner list nodes for memory savings
list-compress-depth 1

# Set optimization: use intset for small integer-only sets
set-max-intset-entries 512

# Sorted set optimization: use ziplist for small sorted sets
zset-max-ziplist-entries 128
zset-max-ziplist-value 64

# HyperLogLog sparse representation threshold
hll-sparse-max-bytes 3000

# Stream node max bytes and entries
stream-node-max-bytes 4096
stream-node-max-entries 100

# Rehashing: use CPU idle time for incremental rehashing
activerehashing yes

# Client output buffer limits
# Normal clients
client-output-buffer-limit normal 0 0 0
# Replica clients
client-output-buffer-limit replica 256mb 64mb 60
# Pub/Sub clients
client-output-buffer-limit pubsub 32mb 8mb 60

# Server Hz: frequency of background tasks (higher = more responsive but more CPU)
hz 10

# Dynamic Hz: adjust based on connected clients
dynamic-hz yes

# Enable incremental AOF rewrite fsync
aof-rewrite-incremental-fsync yes

# Enable incremental RDB save fsync
rdb-save-incremental-fsync yes

# =============================================================================
# ACTIVE DEFRAGMENTATION
# =============================================================================

# Enable active defragmentation for better memory utilization
activedefrag yes

# Start defragmentation when fragmentation exceeds 10%
active-defrag-threshold-lower 10

# Use maximum effort when fragmentation exceeds 100%
active-defrag-threshold-upper 100

# Minimum CPU effort for defragmentation (percentage)
active-defrag-cycle-min 1

# Maximum CPU effort for defragmentation (percentage)
active-defrag-cycle-max 25

# Maximum number of set/hash/zset/list fields processed per defrag cycle
active-defrag-max-scan-fields 1000

# =============================================================================
# TLS/SSL CONFIGURATION (Uncomment and configure for encrypted connections)
# =============================================================================

# tls-port 6380
# tls-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/redis.crt
# tls-key-file /etc/redis/tls/redis.key
# tls-ca-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt
# tls-auth-clients optional
# tls-replication yes
